name: Sun Loan Company
description_short: Sun Loan Company Arlington, Texas — Sun Loan Company offers personal installment loans from $150–$3,999 in Nevada with same-day funding, plus tax prepar...
description_long:
Sun Loan Company is an established personal loan lender with over 30 years of operating history, providing installment loans to consumers in Nevada and select other states. The company operates both online and through physical branch locations, including a Las Vegas branch at 1600 N. Nellis Boulevard. Their Las Vegas location is open Monday–Saturday with Spanish-language services available, reflecting their customer base.

Sun Loan offers personal installment loans ranging from $150 to $3,999.15, designed for debt consolidation, home improvements, medical expenses, major purchases, and emergency costs. They advertise same-day funding options and claim to look beyond credit scores in underwriting decisions. The company also provides tax preparation services, including refund advances up to $7,000 for early filers (by February 28, 2025), plus ancillary services like document copies and fax services.

The company distinguishes itself through customer service emphasis—their website prominently features positive customer testimonials praising staff professionalism and speed. They highlight a simplified application process and the ability to apply online without affecting credit scores. Their dual revenue model (loans + tax services) and bilingual staff support differentiate them from single-product lenders.

However, important caveats exist. The website provides no information on interest rates, APR, fees, or repayment terms—critical metrics for evaluating a personal loan. Loan amounts in Nevada cap at under $4,000, significantly limiting their utility for larger consolidations or purchases. No mention of state licensing, regulatory compliance, or credit reporting practices appears. Customer testimonials are uniformly positive (potentially curated), and the lack of transparent pricing makes independent cost comparison impossible.

As a financial institution, this lender competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans. Many of these lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
address: 2824 S Cooper St Ste 121, Arlington, TX 76015

company_info.headquarters: 2824 S Cooper St Ste 121, Arlington, TX 76015

pros: Same-day funding available when applying in-person at branch locations
Low minimum loan amount ($150) accessible to underbanked consumers
Looks beyond credit score in underwriting decisions, per stated policy
Online application available without credit score impact
Extended branch hours including Saturday service (8:30 AM–12:30 PM select Saturdays)
Tax preparation services integrated with loan offerings, including refund advances up to $7,000
cons: No interest rates, APR, or fee structure disclosed on website—impossible to evaluate true cost
Maximum loan amount of $3,999.15 in Nevada is very limited for meaningful debt consolidation or purchases
No information on state licensing, regulatory oversight, or compliance credentials
Customer testimonials appear curated and universally positive, raising authenticity concerns
Only operates in select states (Nevada, Alabama, Illinois, Missouri, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Texas)—geographic availability is restricted
best_for: Consumers with damaged credit who are rejected by traditional banks and need small emergency funds under $4,000
Nevada residents needing same-day or next-day cash who can visit a physical branch
Bilingual Spanish speakers seeking personal loan and tax services in one location
diagnosis: Sun Loan is best for Nevada residents needing small emergency loans ($150–$3,999) with same-day in-person funding and minimal credit requirements. The critical caveat is complete lack of transparency on interest rates and fees—applicants must visit a branch or call to learn actual borrowing costs, making it impossible to compare this lender against alternatives before committing.
services: In-person loan applications at branch locations
